clause.title: Interior artificial lighting and power control

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: In a Class 4 part of a building, all artificial lighting of a room or space must be individually operated in accordance with Specification 40 by–

subclause.title: NSW REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: This subclause does not apply in NSW.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Subject to (3) to (13), other than in a Class 4 part of a building, all artificial lighting must be controlled by demand-operated control devices in accordance with Specification 40.

subclause.title: NSW REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: Subject to (3) to (12), in a Class 3 building and Class 5 to 9 building all artificial lighting must be controlled by demand-operated control devices in accordance with Specification 40.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A room or space with regular occupancy may have a time switch installed that—

ol.li: overrides demand-operated controls during the period of regular occupancy; and

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A room or space with less than 20 W of artificial lighting that is situated within a larger room or space, may be controlled by manual switch when the artificial lighting within the larger room or space is activated.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A space within a carpark with regular occupancy for greater than 5000 hours per annum need not have demand-operated control.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: For the purposes of (3), (4) and (5) “regular occupancy” is when a room or space is–

ol.li: occupied by more than 1 person per 40 m2; or
used more than once every 15 minutes.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Demand-operated control must be provided in the sole-occupancy unit of a Class 3 building, other than where providing accommodation for people with a disability or the aged, to disable the artificial lighting, air-conditioning, local fans and bathroom heating when the sole-occupancy unit is unoccupied.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A demand-operated control required by (2) must, for other than a single functional space such as an auditorium, theatre, swimming pool, sporting stadium or warehouse, not operate more than 250 m2 of lighting.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: In a Class 5, 6 or 8 building of more than 250 m2, artificial lighting in a natural lighting zone adjacent to windows must be separately controlled from artificial lighting not in a natural lighting zone in the same storey except where—

ol.li: the room containing the natural lighting zone is less than 20 m2; or
the room’s natural lighting zone contains less than 4 luminaires; or
70% or more of the luminaires in the room are in the natural lighting zone.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Artificial lighting in a fire-isolated stairway, fire-isolated passageway or fire-isolated ramp must be controlled by a demand-operated control in accordance with Specification 40.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Artificial lighting in a foyer, corridor and other circulation spaces—

ol.li: of more than 250 W within a single zone; and

subclause.p: must be controlled by a daylight sensor and dynamic lighting control device in accordance with Specification 40.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Artificial lighting for daytime travel in the first 19 m of travel in a carpark entry zone must be controlled by a daylight sensor in accordance with Specification 40.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The requirements of (1) to (12) do not apply to the following:

ol.li: Emergency lighting in accordance with Part E4, including dedicated light sources for photoluminescent exit signs required by S25C3(a).
Where artificial lighting is needed for 24 hour occupancy such as for a manufacturing process, parts of a hospital, an airport control tower or within a detention centre.
